metadata.name: Aluminum
metadata.title: Aluminum Settings
metadata.description: Aluminum serves as a non-ferrous metal in various industrial settings. Its lightweight nature supports applications in aerospace and automotive sectors. The material reduces overall weight in structural components without compromising strength. Corrosion resistance improves longevity in marine and construction environments. Electrical conductivity enables efficient performance in electronics manufacturing. The metal restores flexibility in designs requiring formability, such as packaging and renewable energy structures. Oxidation resistance protects surfaces during exposure to elevated temperatures in food and beverage processing. In rail transport settings, aluminum reveals durability under repeated stress. Adjustments to processing parameters enhance surface finish and reduce internal stresses. Optimal ranges for forming operations minimize defects and improve material integrity. powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Aluminum oxide layer removal

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal Aluminum absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution and energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for Aluminum oxide removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient oxide removal without excessive thermal diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient coverage and cleaning effectiveness

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal pulse overlap for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of aluminum to remove contaminants without melting; starting at 100W balances efficiency and safety. Max limited by aluminum's high reflectivity and thermal conductivity to avoid substrate damage. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on metal cleaning thresholds.

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for light to heavy contamination on aluminum; slower for heavy (e.g., oxide layers), faster for light oils. Recommended 1500 mm/s optimizes removal while minimizing heat buildup due to aluminum's conductivity. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) case studies.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective industrial cleaning; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ but increases cost. 50 ns recommended for aluminum to ablate contaminants with minimal thermal diffusion. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) comparing pulse regimes for metals.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed fiber lasers at 1064 nm; higher rates speed up cleaning but risk heat accumulation on reflective aluminum. 50 kHz balances speed and safety. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021) on frequency effects for aluminum surface preparation.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal; aluminum damage threshold ~4-6 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² ensures safe cleaning below ablation threshold of substrate. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Physical Review B (2017) and Optics Express (2022) for IR laser on aluminum.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for uniform coverage; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. 300 μm recommended for aluminum to achieve even fluence distribution without hotspots. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaners, with optimization in Surface and Coatings Technology (2019).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; aluminum's reflectivity often requires 2 passes for complete removal without damage. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Journal of Laser Applications (2020) on industrial aluminum cleaning.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage and prevents gaps in cleaning; 70% recommended for aluminum to account for beam divergence and material reflectivity. Source: Process optimization studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2018).
